plan_metadata.name: Feature LocalDebug AI Search without AzureOpenAI Keys

steps.description: Click the "Teams Agents and Apps" option in the "New Project" popup within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code, selecting the template for creating agents, bots, web pages, or apps for Microsoft Teams.
Click the "Teams Agent with Data" dropdown option in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension within Visual Studio Code, selecting the agent type for data-driven interactions.
Click the "Azure AI Search" option in the dropdown menu within the "Teams Agent with Data" extension window in Visual Studio Code, selecting it to load data from Azure AI Search service.
Click the "Azure OpenAI" option in the dropdown menu under "Select a service to access LLMs" within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code.
Press the Enter key to confirm the input in the "Input Azure OpenAI service key now or set it later in the project" text box in the Azure OpenAI Key prompt within Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Default Folder" input field in the "Workspace Folder" dialog of Visual Studio Code, within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension.
Press the F1 key in the Visual Studio Code interface with the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension active, to open the command palette for agent development tasks.
Type text: 'Debug: Select and Start Debugging' into the Visual Studio Code command palette input field at the top center of the interface.
Press Enter to select the "Debug: Select and Start Debugging" command in the Visual Studio Code command palette.
Type 'Debug in Teams (Chrome)' into the Visual Studio Code command palette (top-center search box) within the Microsoft 365 Agents Playground extension workspace.
Press the Enter key to select the highlighted "Debug in Teams (Chrome)" option in the Visual Studio Code command palette.
@assertion  'Azure OpenAI Key' is visible
Type text: ${{secret:AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY}} into the Azure OpenAI Key input field in the Visual Studio Code extension interface, located at the top center of the workspace.
Press the Enter key to confirm input in the "Azure OpenAI Key" password field dialog within Visual Studio Code.
Type ${{env:AZURE_OPENAI_MODEL}} into the "Azure OpenAI Deployment Name" input field in the Microsoft 365 Agents extension pane within Visual Studio Code.
Press the 'Enter' key to confirm the Azure OpenAI Deployment Name ("gpt-4.1") input in the Visual Studio Code modal dialog.
Type text ${{env:AZURE_OPENAI_EMBEDDING_DEPLOYMENT_NAME}} into the Azure OpenAI Embedding Deployment Name input field in the Visual Studio Code extension interface.
Press the Enter key to confirm the deployment name "text-embedding-ada-002" in the Azure OpenAI Embedding Deployment Name input dialog within Visual Studio Code.

Type the text ${{secret:AZURE_SEARCH_KEY}} into the input field labeled 'AZURE_SEARCH_KEY' at the top of the Visual Studio Code (VS Code) interface, specifically within the Microsoft 365 Agents extension context.
Press the 'Enter' key to confirm input in the "AZURE_SEARCH_KEY" environment variable dialog in Visual Studio Code.
Click on the "Email, phone, or Skype" input field in the Microsoft Sign in dialog within a web browser, positioning the cursor for user input.

Click the "No" button on the Microsoft "Stay signed in?" dialog within the login page at login.microsoftonline.com. The button is visually identified by a grey background, the word "No", and an adjacent UK flag icon.
Click the "Open" button in the app details popup for vscuse_app_4YhGglocal within the Microsoft Teams web interface.
Click the "Open" button in the vsсuse_app_4YhGglocal app onboarding dialog within the Microsoft Teams web interface.
